candidates.reasoning: The combination of Phoenix canariensis (Canary Island Date Palm) and Mediterranean scrub/pines is highly characteristic of the Spanish coast and islands. This specific landscaping style is ubiquitous in Spanish residential areas.

The presence of Mediterranean palms and hardy evergreen shrubs is consistent with Greek coastal residential zones, sharing the same botanical profile as Spain and Italy.

Portugal also drives on the right and has similar Mediterranean infrastructure, but the specific application of the yellow edge lines seen here is more characteristic of Spain.

The combination of terracotta roof tiles, light-colored stucco walls, and the specific style of concrete block construction for the perimeter walls is very common in Spanish residential areas. The street lamp design is also consistent with many Spanish municipalities.

The architectural style, including the roof tiles and wall finishes, is very similar to Spain. The use of light-colored masonry and the general layout of the residential street are common across the Iberian Peninsula.
